message:
feat(hooks): add command hooks config loader (ntm-spa)

Add config.go to internal/hooks package with:
- CommandEvent type and 10 event constants (pre/post-spawn, send, add, etc.)
- CommandHook struct with command, timeout, workdir, env support
- CommandHooksConfig with event-based hook filtering
- TOML config loading from hooks.toml and main config.toml
- Duration type for TOML duration parsing
- ExpandWorkDir with ${SESSION} and ${PROJECT} variable substitution
- Comprehensive test coverage (config_test.go)

This unblocks ntm-85b (hook executor with timeout and error handling).

Example hooks.toml config:
```toml
[[command_hooks]]
event = "pre-spawn"
command = "git stash"
timeout = "30s"
description = "Stash changes before spawning"
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-08r","title":"Implement ntm mail send command","description":"# Implement ntm mail send Command

## Purpose

Allow users to send messages between agents in a session, or broadcast coordination messages.

## Command Syntax

```bash
# Send to specific agent panes
ntm mail send myproject --to cc_1 "Please review the API changes"

# Send to all agents of a type
ntm mail send myproject --cc "Stop current work and focus on tests"

# Send to all agents
ntm mail send myproject --all "Checkpoint: sync and report status"

# With options
ntm mail send myproject --to cc_1 --subject "API Review" --urgent --ack-required "Review needed ASAP"

# From file
ntm mail send myproject --to cc_1 --file ./message.md
```

## Flag Mapping

| Flag | Agent Mail Parameter |
|------|---------------------|
| --to | to[] (specific agent) |
| --cc | cc[] |
| --subject | subject |
| --urgent | importance="urgent" |
| --important | importance="high" |
| --ack-required | ack_required=true |
| --thread | thread_id |
| --file | body_md from file |

## Agent Resolution

The --to flag accepts:
1. **Pane identifiers**: cc_1, cod_2 → resolve to agent names
2. **Agent names**: GreenCastle → use directly
3. **Agent type filters**: --cc, --cod, --gmi → expand to all agents of type

Resolution requires querying both tmux (for pane names) and Agent Mail (for registered agents).

## Message Composition

If no message body provided:
1. Open $EDITOR with template
2. Parse subject from first line (# heading)
3. Validate non-empty before sending

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Can send to specific agents by pane ID or name
- [ ] Can broadcast to agent type groups
- [ ] Subject auto-derived or manually specified
- [ ] Importance and ack_required flags work
- [ ] Thread ID support for conversations
- [ ] File input for long messages
- [ ] Editor mode for composition","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:45.793913-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:33:42.490087-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-08r","depends_on_id":"ntm-yxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.852522-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-0d7","title":"Implement ntm mail ack and read commands","description":"# Implement ntm mail ack and read Commands

## Purpose

Allow marking messages as read or acknowledged, which signals to senders that the message was received and processed.

## Command Syntax

```bash
# Mark message as read
ntm mail read myproject 42

# Mark message as acknowledged
ntm mail ack myproject 42

# Acknowledge all urgent messages
ntm mail ack myproject --urgent

# Acknowledge all from specific agent
ntm mail ack myproject --from cc_1
```

## Semantics

- **Read**: Message was seen but not necessarily acted upon
- **Acknowledged**: Message was seen AND the recipient has taken action or committed to action

Agent Mail tracks both timestamps separately:
- read_ts: When mark_message_read was called
- ack_ts: When acknowledge_message was called

## Batch Operations

```bash
# Mark multiple messages
ntm mail read myproject 42 43 44

# Mark all unread as read
ntm mail read myproject --all

# Acknowledge all pending
ntm mail ack myproject --all
```

## Output

```
✓ Message #42 marked as read
✓ Message #42 acknowledged (ack_ts: 2025-12-10T15:35:00Z)
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single message read/ack by ID
- [ ] Batch operations by ID list
- [ ] Filter-based batch: --urgent, --from, --all
- [ ] Proper error handling for already-read/acked
- [ ] JSON output mode for scripts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:49.63059-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:49.63059-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-0d7","depends_on_id":"ntm-161","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:56.022722-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-0qz","title":"Unit Tests: internal/palette package (0% → 60%)","description":"# Unit Tests: internal/palette Package

## Current State
- Coverage: 0.0%
- Purpose: Command palette TUI with fuzzy search and animations

## Test Approach
Bubble Tea models are tested by:
1. Init() - verify initial state
2. Update(msg) - verify state transitions
3. View() - verify output contains expected elements

TUI tests are harder to make deterministic - target 60% coverage.

## Test Cases

### model.go

#### Initialization
- [ ] TestNewModel - creates model with default state
- [ ] TestNewModelWithCommands - loads commands correctly
- [ ] TestInitialPhase - starts in PhaseCommand

#### Phase Transitions
- [ ] TestPhaseCommandToTarget - selecting command moves to target phase
- [ ] TestPhaseTargetToConfirm - selecting target sends and exits
- [ ] TestPhaseBack - Escape returns to previous phase

#### Command Filtering
- [ ] TestFilterByLabel - fuzzy match on label
- [ ] TestFilterByKey - fuzzy match on key
- [ ] TestFilterByCategory - fuzzy match on category
- [ ] TestFilterClearOnBackspace - clearing filter shows all
- [ ] TestFilterNoMatches - empty results handled

#### Navigation
- [ ] TestNavigateDown - j/down moves selection
- [ ] TestNavigateUp - k/up moves selection
- [ ] TestNavigateWrap - wraps at boundaries
- [ ] TestQuickSelect - 1-9 selects item

#### Target Selection
- [ ] TestTargetAll - selects all agents
- [ ] TestTargetClaude - selects Claude only
- [ ] TestTargetCodex - selects Codex only
- [ ] TestTargetGemini - selects Gemini only

### Animation (lower priority)
- [ ] TestShimmerEffect - animates on tick
- [ ] TestAnimationTick - tick updates animation state

## View Output Tests
- [ ] TestViewContainsBanner - banner rendered
- [ ] TestViewContainsCommands - command list rendered
- [ ] TestViewContainsPreview - preview pane rendered
- [ ] TestViewContainsKeyHints - keyboard hints shown

## Implementation Notes
- Use tea.Batch for message simulation
- Capture View() output and assert contains
- Animation tests may be flaky, mark appropriately","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:42.477205-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:42.477205-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-0qz","depends_on_id":"ntm-idx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:22:34.045222-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-0wf","title":"E2E Test: Robot Mode JSON Output","description":"# E2E Test: Robot Mode JSON Output

## Scenario
Verify all --robot-* flags produce valid JSON that AI agents can consume.

## Test Cases

### Test 1: --robot-status
- Create various session states
- Run ntm --robot-status
- Parse JSON
- Verify all fields present and accurate
- Log full JSON output

### Test 2: --robot-plan
- Create sessions with different states
- Run ntm --robot-plan
- Verify suggested actions make sense
- Log recommendations

### Test 3: --robot-version
- Run ntm --robot-version
- Verify version/commit/date fields
- Log version info

### Test 4: --robot-help
- Run ntm --robot-help
- Verify documentation present
- Log help content

## Schema Validation
Define and validate JSON schemas:
```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["generated_at", "sessions", "summary"],
  "properties": {
    "generated_at": {"type": "string", "format": "date-time"},
    "sessions": {"type": "array"},
    "summary": {"type": "object"}
  }
}
```

## Logging
Include full JSON output in logs for debugging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:59.020495-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:59.020495-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-0wf","depends_on_id":"ntm-66c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:42.235374-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-0wf","depends_on_id":"ntm-vr2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:43.313959-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-161","title":"Implement ntm mail inbox command","description":"# Implement ntm mail inbox Command

## Purpose

Display an AGGREGATE inbox view showing messages across ALL agents in the project, not just NTM's coordinator messages.

## IMPORTANT: Aggregate View

This is not just NTM's inbox. It shows:
1. Messages TO any agent in the project (GreenCastle, BlueLake, etc.)
2. Messages FROM any agent in the project
3. Filtered by session agents if desired

This gives the user visibility into all agent-to-agent communication.

## Command Syntax

```bash
# Show all messages in project (across all agents)
ntm mail inbox myproject

# Filter to session's agents only
ntm mail inbox myproject --session-agents

# Filter to specific agent
ntm mail inbox myproject --agent GreenCastle

# Urgent only
ntm mail inbox myproject --urgent

# JSON for scripts
ntm mail inbox myproject --json
```

## Output Format

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Project Inbox: myproject (5 agents)                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● #42 [URGENT] API Review Needed                            │
│   GreenCastle → BlueLake                                    │
│   2 minutes ago │ Thread: FEAT-123 │ ⚠️ Ack required         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ○ #41 Tests passing, ready for merge                        │
│   RedStone → GreenCastle, BlueLake                          │
│   15 minutes ago │ Thread: FEAT-123                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ○ #40 Starting work on src/api/**                           │
│   ntm_myproject → all                                       │
│   30 minutes ago │ (coordinator announcement)               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Showing messages involving: GreenCastle, BlueLake, RedStone, ntm_myproject
```

## Implementation

1. Discover all agents in project via Agent Mail
2. Fetch inbox for EACH agent
3. Deduplicate (same message may appear in multiple inboxes)
4. Sort by timestamp
5. Display with sender → recipient format

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shows messages across ALL project agents
- [ ] Deduplication (message shown once even if multiple recipients)
- [ ] Filter by --session-agents, --agent, --urgent
- [ ] Clear indication of sender → recipient
- [ ] Coordinator messages distinguished from agent messages
- [ ] --json output for scripts","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:48.350455-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:01:19.314483-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-161","depends_on_id":"ntm-yxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.9288-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-8h3","title":"EPIC: Automation, Pipelines & Integration","description":"Advanced automation including agent pipelines, webhooks, and external integrations.

## Problem Statement
NTM is currently manual and reactive:
- Each prompt requires explicit user action
- No way to chain agent outputs as inputs to other agents
- No notifications when agents finish or error
- No integration with external tools (CI, Slack, etc.)
- File content must be manually copied into prompts

## Goals
1. Enable pipeline mode (chain agents sequentially)
2. Support webhook/notification on events
3. Easy file content injection into prompts
4. Token/cost estimation before sending expensive prompts

## Success Criteria
- `ntm pipeline` chains agent stages with output passing
- Config supports on_error, on_complete webhooks
- `ntm send --file=path` injects file content
- `ntm send --estimate` shows token/cost estimate

## Pipeline Mode
```bash
ntm pipeline myproject \
  --stage="cc: Design the authentication module" \
  --stage="cod: Implement the design from previous stage" \
  --stage="gmi: Write tests for the implementation"
```

Each stage:
1. Sends prompt to specified agent type
2. Waits for completion (detect idle state)
3. Captures output
4. Passes output as context to next stage

## Webhook Config
```toml
[notifications]
on_error = "curl -X POST https://slack.webhook/..."
on_complete = "terminal-notifier -message 'Done'"
on_rate_limit = "echo 'Rate limited' >> ~/ntm-alerts.log"
```

## File Context
```bash
ntm send myproject --all \
  --file=src/auth.py \
  --file=src/models.py:10-50 \
  "Refactor these files to use dependency injection"
```

Injects files with formatting:
```
# File: src/auth.py
\`\`\`python
<file contents>
\`\`\`
```

## Cost Estimation
- Count tokens in prompt using tiktoken or similar
- Estimate based on known pricing (Claude, Codex, Gemini)
- Show estimate, ask for confirmation on expensive prompts

## User Value
- Sophisticated multi-stage workflows
- Stay informed without constant monitoring
- Faster prompt construction with file injection
- Budget awareness for API-heavy usage","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:38:05.291885-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:38:05.291885-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-8h3.1","title":"File Context Injection (--file flag)","description":"Add --file flag to ntm send to inject file contents into prompts.

## Use Case
Instead of manually copying file contents, specify files to include:
```bash
ntm send myproject --all \
  --file=src/auth.py \
  --file=src/models.py \
  "Refactor these to use dependency injection"
```

## Prompt Injection Format
Files are prepended to prompt with clear formatting:
```
# File: src/auth.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/auth.py>
\`\`\`

# File: src/models.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/models.py>
\`\`\`

---

Refactor these to use dependency injection
```

## Extended Syntax
```bash
--file=path                    # Entire file
--file=path:10-50              # Lines 10-50
--file=path:10-                # Line 10 to end
--file=path:-50                # First 50 lines
--file=path:func:my_function   # Extract function (future)
```

## Implementation
```go
type FileSpec struct {
    Path      string
    StartLine int  // 0 = from beginning
    EndLine   int  // 0 = to end
}

func ParseFileSpec(spec string) (FileSpec, error) {
    // Parse "path:start-end" format
}

func InjectFiles(specs []FileSpec, prompt string) (string, error) {
    var parts []string
    
    for _, spec := range specs {
        content, err := readFileRange(spec)
        if err != nil {
            return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read %s: %w", spec.Path, err)
        }
        
        lang := detectLanguage(spec.Path)
        header := fmt.Sprintf("# File: %s", spec.Path)
        if spec.StartLine > 0 || spec.EndLine > 0 {
            header += fmt.Sprintf(" (lines %d-%d)", spec.StartLine, spec.EndLine)
        }
        
        parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s\n```%s\n%s\n```", 
            header, lang, content))
    }
    
    parts = append(parts, "---\n\n"+prompt)
    return strings.Join(parts, "\n\n"), nil
}
```

## Language Detection
Map file extensions to markdown language tags:
```go
var langMap = map[string]string{
    ".py":   "python",
    ".go":   "go",
    ".js":   "javascript",
    ".ts":   "typescript",
    ".rs":   "rust",
    ".rb":   "ruby",
    ".java": "java",
    // etc.
}
```

## Integration with Templates
Templates can reference {{file}} and {{files}}:
```markdown
---
name: review
variables:
  file: required
---
Review the following file for issues:

{{file}}

Focus on security and performance.
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| -f, --file | File to include (repeatable) |
| --file-format | Format: markdown (default), raw, xml |
| --no-file-header | Omit file path headers |

## Edge Cases
- File not found: Error with clear message
- Binary files: Skip with warning
- Very large files: Warn if > 50KB, --force to override
- Relative vs absolute paths: Resolve relative to cwd

## Files to Modify
- internal/cli/send.go - Add --file flag processing
- internal/prompt/inject.go - File injection logic (new)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-8h3.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-8h3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.520574-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"ntm-8js","title":"Implement ntm personas list/show commands","description":"# Implement ntm personas list/show Commands

## Purpose

Allow users to discover and inspect available personas.

## Commands

```bash
# List all personas
ntm personas list
ntm personas list --json

# Show specific persona
ntm personas show architect
ntm personas show architect --json
```

## List Output

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Available Personas                                         │
├──────────────┬────────────┬────────────────────────────────┤
│ Name         │ Agent      │ Description                    │
├──────────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
│ architect    │ claude     │ Senior software architect      │
│ implementer  │ claude     │ Fast implementation engineer   │
│ reviewer     │ claude     │ Meticulous code reviewer       │
│ tester       │ codex      │ Test engineer                  │
│ documenter   │ gemini     │ Documentation writer           │
├──────────────┴────────────┴────────────────────────────────┤
│ Source: 3 built-in, 2 user (~/.config/ntm/personas.toml)   │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Show Output

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Persona: architect                                         │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Agent Type:   claude                                       │
│ Model:        opus                                         │
│ Temperature:  0.7                                          │
│ Source:       built-in                                     │
│ Tags:         design, review                               │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ System Prompt:                                             │
│                                                            │
│ You are a senior software architect. Focus on:             │
│ - System design and high-level structure                   │
│ - Reviewing and guiding implementation                     │
│ - Identifying architectural risks                          │
│ - NOT writing implementation code (delegate that)          │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Context Files:                                             │
│ - ARCHITECTURE.md                                          │
│ - docs/design/*.md                                         │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## JSON Output

```json
{
  "name": "architect",
  "description": "Senior software architect",
  "agent_type": "claude",
  "model": "opus",
  "temperature": 0.7,
  "system_prompt": "...",
  "context_files": ["ARCHITECTURE.md", "docs/design/*.md"],
  "tags": ["design", "review"],
  "source": "built-in"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm personas list shows all personas
- [ ] ntm personas show displays details
- [ ] Source indicator (built-in, user, project)
- [ ] --json output for scripts
- [ ] Filtering by tag or agent type","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:49.772792-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:49.772792-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-8js","depends_on_id":"ntm-hzl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:01.048364-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-ctt","title":"Add --json support to spawn, create, add, send commands","description":"Implement JSON output for commands that perform actions.

## What This Task Does

Add --json support to commands that modify state or perform actions:
- ntm spawn: Return session details after creation
- ntm create: Return session info after creation  
- ntm add: Return pane info for newly added agents
- ntm send: Return delivery confirmation for sent prompts

## Why These Commands

These commands are commonly used in scripts and automation:
- CI/CD pipelines spawn sessions and need session IDs
- Scripts add agents and need pane indices
- Automated workflows send prompts and need confirmation

## JSON Schemas

### ntm spawn --json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "created": true,
  "working_directory": "/path/to/project",
  "panes": [
    {"index": 0, "agent_type": "claude", "title": "myproject__cc_1"},
    {"index": 1, "agent_type": "codex", "title": "myproject__cod_1"}
  ],
  "agent_counts": {"claude": 2, "codex": 1}
}

### ntm send --json  
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "prompt_preview": "First 50 chars of prompt...",
  "targets": [0, 1, 2],
  "delivered": 3,
  "failed": 0
}

## Implementation

1. Create output structs for each command
2. Modify command to build struct before any output
3. Use output formatter based on --json flag
4. Ensure errors also return valid JSON when flag is set

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm spawn --json returns session details
- [ ] ntm create --json returns session info
- [ ] ntm add --json returns new pane info
- [ ] ntm send --json returns delivery confirmation
- [ ] Errors produce valid JSON with error field

 {"id":"ntm-cx7","title":"Add --json support to ntm list command","description":"Implement JSON output for the list command.

## What This Task Does

Modify the ntm list command to support --json flag for structured output.

## JSON Schema

{
  "sessions": [
    {
      "name": "myproject",
      "created_at": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z",
      "attached": false,
      "pane_count": 4,
      "agents": {
        "claude": 2,
        "codex": 1,
        "gemini": 1
      },
      "working_directory": "/path/to/project"
    }
  ],
  "count": 1
}

## Implementation

1. Import internal/output package
2. Create SessionListOutput struct
3. Modify runList to build struct instead of print directly
4. Use formatter to output based on format flag
5. Ensure all existing text output information is in JSON

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm list --json produces valid JSON
- [ ] JSON includes all session information
- [ ] Agent counts are structured as object
- [ ] Empty list produces {"sessions": [], "count": 0}
- [ ] Text output unchanged (default behavior)

 {"id":"ntm-cyq","title":"Implement token estimation library","description":"# Implement Token Estimation Library

## Purpose

Provide rough token estimates for context usage visualization.

## IMPORTANT: Estimation, Not Measurement

We're ESTIMATING tokens, not measuring actual usage. Be honest about this.

## Approach: Character-Based Heuristic

Simpler and more honest than pretending to have accurate tokenizers:

```go
package tokens

// EstimateTokens provides a rough token count estimate.
// Uses ~3.5 characters per token heuristic for English text.
// This is an ESTIMATE - actual token counts vary by model and content.
func EstimateTokens(text string) int {
    // ~3.5 chars per token for typical English
    return len(text) * 10 / 35
}

// EstimateWithOverhead applies overhead multiplier for hidden context.
// Overhead includes: system prompts, tool definitions, conversation structure.
func EstimateWithOverhead(visibleText string, multiplier float64) int {
    visible := EstimateTokens(visibleText)
    return int(float64(visible) * multiplier)
}
```

## Model Context Limits

Approximate limits (may change, conservative estimates):
```go
var ContextLimits = map[string]int{
    "opus":   200000,
    "sonnet": 200000,
    "haiku":  200000,
    "gpt4":   128000,
    "o1":     128000,
    "pro":    1000000,
    "flash":  1000000,
}

func GetContextLimit(model string) int {
    if limit, ok := ContextLimits[normalizeModel(model)]; ok {
        return limit
    }
    return 128000 // Conservative default
}
```

## Why Not Use tiktoken?

1. tiktoken is for OpenAI tokenizers - Claude uses different tokenization
2. Each model family has different tokenizers
3. Character heuristic is 80% accurate and works for ALL models
4. We're showing estimates anyway - precision is false confidence

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] EstimateTokens function with character heuristic
- [ ] EstimateWithOverhead for total context estimate
- [ ] Context limit lookup by model
- [ ] Clear documentation that these are estimates
- [ ] Unit tests validating reasonable estimates","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireRiver","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:08.230758-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:00:32.487543-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T04:00:32.487543-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-sc6","title":"Implement agent progress detection","description":"Detect and report agent progress through work items.

## Rationale
When an agent is working on a task, I need to understand progress:
- Is it just starting?
- Is it midway through?
- Is it finishing up?
- Has it encountered a blocker?

This enables smarter orchestration (don't interrupt agents near completion, reassign stuck work, etc.)

## Progress Indicators
Detect progress from pane output patterns:

### Starting Indicators
- 'Let me', 'I'll', 'Starting'
- File reads, searches
- Planning/analysis output

### Working Indicators
- File edits
- Code blocks being written
- Test runs
- Build commands

### Finishing Indicators
- 'Done', 'Complete', 'Finished'
- Summary output
- Return to prompt
- Commit messages

### Stuck Indicators
- 'Error', 'Failed', 'Cannot'
- Repeated attempts
- Questions asked
- Long pauses

## Output in Snapshot
```json
{
  "agents": [{
    "pane": "0.1",
    "progress": {
      "stage": "working",     // starting, working, finishing, stuck
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "indicators": ["file_edits", "code_blocks"],
      "time_in_stage_sec": 120
    }
  }]
}
```

## Future: ML-based Detection
Could train a classifier on agent outputs to detect progress more accurately. For now, heuristic-based is sufficient.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:19.308214-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:19.308214-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-sim","title":"Implement --robot-snapshot for unified state query","description":"Core orchestration primitive: single command returns complete system state including all sessions, agent health, bead summary, mail status, and alerts. This is THE most important flag because it eliminates the need for multiple queries.

## Rationale
When orchestrating N agents across M sessions working on P beads with Q mail threads, I currently need separate queries for each dimension. This compounds latency (4-6 tool calls) and burns tokens on redundant parsing. A unified snapshot gives me complete situational awareness in one atomic read.

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "ts": "ISO8601",
  "sessions": [{
    "name": "string",
    "agents": [{
      "pane": "window.pane",
      "type": "claude|codex|gemini|cursor|windsurf|aider",
      "state": "active|idle|stuck|waiting",
      "last_output_age_sec": number,
      "output_tail_lines": number,
      "current_bead": "bead-id|null",
      "pending_mail": number
    }]
  }],
  "beads_summary": {"open": N, "in_progress": N, "blocked": N, "ready": N},
  "mail_unread": number,
  "alerts": ["string"]
}
```

## Key Design Decisions
- Always include everything (no optional fields that require follow-up)
- Use compact field names (abbreviations OK for frequently-accessed data)
- Include computed fields (last_output_age_sec vs raw timestamp)
- Alerts are pre-filtered to actionable items only

## Integration Points
- tmux: session/pane enumeration, output capture
- beads: bd list --json integration  
- agent-mail: inbox count per agent
- pane title parsing for agent type detection","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:07.458116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:34.255328-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:20:34.255328-05:00","comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"ntm-sim","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Implementation Architecture

### File Structure
```
internal/robot/
├── robot.go          # Existing - add Snapshot type and function
├── snapshot.go       # NEW - SnapshotOutput, BuildSnapshot()
├── integrations.go   # NEW - BeadsIntegration, MailIntegration
└── alerts.go         # NEW - AlertManager, Alert types
```

### Core Type
```go
type SnapshotOutput struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time              `json:"ts"`
    Sessions    []SessionSnapshot      `json:"sessions"`
    Beads       *BeadsSnapshot         `json:"beads,omitempty"`
    AgentMail   *MailSnapshot          `json:"agent_mail,omitempty"`
    Alerts      []Alert                `json:"alerts"`
    Summary     SnapshotSummary        `json:"summary"`
}
```

### Flag Registration
In root.go init():
```go
rootCmd.Flags().Bool("robot-snapshot", false, "Output complete system state as JSON")
rootCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Return only changes since timestamp (ISO8601)")
```

### Performance Target
- Cold snapshot: <100ms
- With caching: <20ms
- Memory: <5MB for 10 sessions with 50 panes","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:55:34Z"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-t0ch","title":"Implement file conflict detection","description":"# Implement File Conflict Detection

## Purpose

Detect when multiple agents modify the same file, potentially creating conflicts.

## Detection Logic

```go
type Conflict struct {
    Path   string
    Agents []AgentChange
}

func DetectConflicts(changes []AgentChange) []Conflict {
    byPath := make(map[string][]AgentChange)
    for _, c := range changes {
        byPath[c.File] = append(byPath[c.File], c)
    }
    
    var conflicts []Conflict
    for path, agents := range byPath {
        if len(agents) > 1 {
            conflicts = append(conflicts, Conflict{
                Path:   path,
                Agents: agents,
            })
        }
    }
    return conflicts
}
```

## Severity Levels

- **Warning**: Same file modified by 2+ agents (may or may not conflict)
- **Critical**: Same lines modified (if we can detect via git diff)

## Integration with ntm send --all

After broadcast completes:
```
ntm send myproject --all "Implement the feature"
# ... agents work ...
# On completion:
⚠️ Potential conflict: src/api/users.py modified by cc_1, cc_2
  Run `ntm conflicts myproject` for details
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detect same-file modifications across agents
- [ ] Warn user after broadcasts
- [ ] ntm conflicts command shows details
- [ ] Severity classification (warning vs critical)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:14.387332-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:40:18.276326-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-t0ch","depends_on_id":"ntm-xkvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:37.60962-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
